Life Physics Reflection

Repost: Midterm season is over!

January 24, 2026

Midterms didn't go too bad. Probably would get grades lower than what I wanted, but it wasn't terrible.

Now that midterms are over, it means that school is kind of reset, which means I won't get much homework, hopefully. During the midterms I really wanted to study physics, and I was really sad I could not study physics. Like I spent the entire day studying midterm for the next day, while also attending robotics meetings for a while. It was crazy schedule, and after sleeping for 4 hours for 5 days straight, I got so tired and my nose started to randomly bleed a lot. So first thing I did after taking the last midterm was taking a nap.

I really need to focus on physics now, and hopefully finish hrk by end of this weekend, or even on Monday. If I don't finish by Monday, I'm quite literally cooked. Even if I do finish on Monday, I would have like 15 days until f=ma, and considering I do a mock every day + review, that's like 3 hrs already, and maybe blue morin for an hour every day and like 5hrs on weekends? I honestly don't know. I got like 18 days, and hopefully I do these things.

I just skimmed through 2008 f=ma (since recent tests are more valuable so I don't want to get spoiled), and this seems actually easy. I might make it. Memorizing equations might be a pain though. I also have to buy a scientific calculator for f=ma, so give me a calculator rec, I might buy it.

Comments
